title: The Baker Who Works at Night
summary: A night baker in Lyon loves the quiet hours that no one else sees.
body:
Mathilde is a baker in Lyon, France. She starts work at two in the morning. The city is dark and quiet. Most people are asleep in their warm beds. Some people feel sorry for her. They say, "Your job is so hard. You never see the sun." But Mathilde does not feel sad. She likes the night.

In the bakery, the air is warm. The smell of fresh bread is everywhere. Mathilde works alone, but she is not lonely. She thinks about the people who will eat her bread. A tired nurse will buy a roll after a long night. A child will eat warm bread before school.

At six in the morning, the first customers come. They are happy and hungry. "Your bread is the best," they say. Mathilde smiles. The sun comes up, and her work is done. She walks home while the city wakes up.

People think the best part of her day is the morning, when everyone praises her. But Mathilde says the best part is two in the morning. In those quiet hours, the bread is hers alone. No one is watching. It is just her, the warm oven, and the soft dark.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về thói quen hằng ngày. Động từ thêm '-s' với chủ ngữ số ít.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use the present simple for daily habits, as in "She starts work at two in the morning."

grammarNotes.title: but to show contrast
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'but' nối hai ý trái ngược nhau.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: "but" joins two opposite ideas, as in "She works alone, but she is not lonely."

grammarNotes.title: Time phrases with 'at' and 'in'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'at' với giờ cụ thể và 'in' với buổi trong ngày.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We say "at two in the morning" and "in the morning" to talk about time.

translationVi:
Mathilde là một thợ làm bánh ở Lyon, Pháp. Cô bắt đầu làm việc lúc hai giờ sáng. Thành phố tối và yên tĩnh. Hầu hết mọi người đang ngủ trong những chiếc giường ấm áp. Một số người thấy thương cho cô. Họ nói: "Công việc của cô vất vả quá. Cô chẳng bao giờ thấy mặt trời." Nhưng Mathilde không thấy buồn. Cô thích ban đêm.

Trong tiệm bánh, không khí ấm áp. Mùi bánh mì mới ra lò ở khắp nơi. Mathilde làm việc một mình, nhưng cô không cô đơn. Cô nghĩ về những người sẽ ăn bánh của cô. Một cô y tá mệt mỏi sẽ mua một ổ bánh nhỏ sau một đêm dài. Một đứa trẻ sẽ ăn bánh mì ấm trước khi đến trường.

Lúc sáu giờ sáng, những khách hàng đầu tiên đến. Họ vui vẻ và đói bụng. "Bánh của cô ngon nhất," họ nói. Mathilde mỉm cười. Mặt trời mọc lên, và công việc của cô đã xong. Cô đi bộ về nhà trong khi thành phố thức dậy.

Mọi người nghĩ phần hay nhất trong ngày của cô là buổi sáng, khi ai cũng khen cô. Nhưng Mathilde nói phần hay nhất là lúc hai giờ sáng. Trong những giờ yên tĩnh đó, mẻ bánh là của riêng cô. Không ai nhìn. Chỉ có cô, chiếc lò ấm, và bóng đêm dịu dàng.

questions.explanation: The text says she is "a baker in Lyon, France."

questions.prompt: What time does she start work?
questions.explanation: "She starts work at two in the morning."

questions.prompt: What is the city like when she starts?
Why do some people feel sorry for her?
questions.explanation: They say "You never see the sun."
questions.hint: Look at what people say to her.

questions.prompt: What is the smell in the bakery?
questions.explanation: "The smell of fresh bread is everywhere."

questions.prompt: Who buys a roll after a long night?
questions.explanation: "A tired nurse will buy a roll after a long night."
questions.hint: Look at the people she thinks about.

questions.prompt: When do the first customers come?
questions.explanation: "At six in the morning, the first customers come."

questions.prompt: What does the word "lonely" mean in the passage?
questions.explanation: "She works alone, but she is not lonely" shows lonely means feeling sad when alone.
questions.hint: Look at the word next to "alone."

questions.prompt: Why is Mathilde not lonely while she works?
options: She thinks about the people who will eat her bread
questions.explanation: "She thinks about the people who will eat her bread."

questions.prompt: What does Mathilde say is the best part of her day?
questions.explanation: "Mathilde says the best part is two in the morning."

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: She finds quiet joy in work others pity
questions.explanation: Others pity her, but she loves the quiet hours when "the bread is hers alone."
questions.hint: Think about the whole story, not one line.

questions.prompt: How does Mathilde feel about the night?

blanks.explanation: She makes bread, so she is a baker.
"two in the morning" is the fixed time phrase.
People are asleep in their beds at night.
"feel sorry for her" means they pity her.
"the air is warm" from the oven.
"she is not lonely" even though she is alone.
"A tired nurse will buy a roll."
"the first customers come" to buy bread.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mathilde says the best part of her day is two in the morning, when 'no one is watching.' Why might a person enjoy work more when no one praises them?
